headline: sbi likely to raise up to rs 7500 crore via tier 2 bonds
body: Synopsis  State Bank of India plans to raise up to ₹7,500 crore through tier-2 bonds next week to meet growing credit demand. The bond sale, with a base size of ₹3,500-4,000 crore and the remainder via a greenshoe option, is set for September 18. The funds will likely support regulatory requirements and business growth. headline: Should investors buy TCS, Infosys ahead of Q4 results or AI risk too big to ignore?
body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

A report by State Bank of India notes that historically, such growth has rarely coincided with a rate cut in India or elsewhere.  Economists at SBI have detected an intriguing aspect in the RBI's latest projection of 7 per cent GDP growth for the financial year 2024-25, and issued a report flagging a likely occurrence that has happened "rarely, if ever, either in India or globally".

body: Synopsis  Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd (APSEZ) handled 36 million metric tonnes (MMT) of cargo in November 2024, with the container segment showing a 21% growth. headline: hdfc flexi cap fund turned rs 1 lakh lumpsum investment into rs 2 crore in 30 years

headline: ongc shares in focus after petrochemical arm exits from dahej sez eyes local market
body: Shares of ONGC will be in focus on Monday after its subsidiary ONGC PetroAdditions Ltd (OPaL) decided to relinquish its "only-for-export" status and shift its focus to the rapidly expanding domestic petrochemical market. The company has received final approval to exit the Dahej Special Economic Zone (SEZ), and will now operate as a Domestic Tariff Area (DTA) unit, effective from March 8, 2025. headline: airtel working on tech to wipe out scam phishing
body: Synopsis  Bharti Airtel is developing technology to prevent online scams and phishing attempts for its 380 million mobile users. headline: infosys among other it stocks slide up to 4 as recession fears weigh on us market
body: IT stocks, including Coforge and Infosys, dropped by as much as 4% in early trading on Tuesday, March 11, as recession fears surrounding the U.S. economy weighed on market sentiment.  Shares of Coforge and Infosys fell 4% to Rs 7,223.90 and Rs 1637.05 respectively, while Wipro shares fell 2.8% to a low of Rs 273.10. headline: what could trigger a bounce back in reliance stock from a 52 week low mayuresh joshi explains
body: Mayuresh Joshi, Head Equity, Marketsmith India, says three triggers could lift Reliance out of a 52-week low. One, any de-merger news of either the telecom or the retail operations might act as a trigger for the stock. Second, the Street would like to see if the telecom ARPU increases can be sustained going forward. That will create a huge leverage in the individual telecom balance sheet, as well as retail. headline: Should investors buy TCS, Infosys ahead of Q4 results or AI risk too big to ignore?
body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

body: Gautam Adani-led Adani Enterprises on Thursday reported a 752% year-on-year (YoY) surge in profit to Rs 3,845 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2025, compared to Rs 451 crore in the same period last year. headline: tata motors total sales down 6 to 72753 units in april
body: Synopsis  Tata Motors saw a 6.1% decrease in overall sales, totaling 72,753 units in April 2025, compared to 77,521 units in April 2024. Domestic sales also saw a decline of 7%, reaching 70,963 units. Passenger vehicle sales, including EVs, fell by 5%, while commercial vehicle sales dropped by 8% during the same period.  Tata Motors Ltd on Thursday reported a 6.1 per cent decline in overall sales to 72,753 units in April 2025. The company posted the total sales at 77,521 units during April 2024.

headline: adani ports q4 results net profit jumps 37 to a record in fy25
body: Mumbai: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone clocked in a 50% year-on-year growth in its consolidated profit for the March quarter at ₹3,023 crore, aided by a 23% jump in revenue to ₹8,488 crore.  Most brokerages had pegged bottomline for the company in the range of ₹2,600-2,700 crore.  For the full year (April 2024-March 2025), Adani Ports' profit rose 37% on-year to an all-time high of ₹11,061 crore. This was driven by growth across its ports, logistics and marine business, the company said.

The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

headline: Should investors buy TCS, Infosys ahead of Q4 results or AI risk too big to ignore?
body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

headline: rbi rate cut in october mpc meeting looks unlikely as inflation set to rise in august sbi report
body: Synopsis  The Reserve Bank of India is unlikely to cut interest rates in October due to expected inflation rise in August 2025, potentially delaying any rate cut until December. India's retail inflation hit a 98-month low in July 2025, driven by falling food and core inflation.  The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is unlikely to cut interest rates in its October policy meeting, as inflation in August 2025 is expected to rise above 2 per cent, according to a report by the State Bank of India (SBI).

headline: Should investors buy TCS, Infosys ahead of Q4 results or AI risk too big to ignore?
body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh

headline: tata motors posts record 60907 september sales on gst cut and navratri demand

body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. headline: reliance halts cell making plans after failed bid for china tech
body: Synopsis  Reliance Industries has paused its lithium-ion battery cell manufacturing plans in India after failing to secure Chinese technology, highlighting supply chain challenges. The company is now focusing on assembling battery energy storage systems, a move mirrored by other Indian conglomerates facing similar technology hurdles. headline: Should investors buy TCS, Infosys ahead of Q4 results or AI risk too big to ignore?
body: Indian IT stocks are heading into the March quarter earnings season under pressure, with the Nifty IT index down nearly 20% this year. The key question for investors is whether the correction offers a buying opportunity ahead of the results or if structural concerns around demand and artificial intelligence (AI) are still too large to ignore.  Brokerages and analysts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, pointing to weak global demand and limited discretionary spending by clients. Ravi Singh
